metadata.title: Quick-fire: the one and the many
metadata.author: patterns-of-choice editorial draft (AI)
metadata.design_intent: Abstract (de-attached) twin items for the H8 high-stakes paired probe pp-allocation-001. Item i01 is the structural twin of the narrative 'High water' rescue choice; the rest are same-construct one-vs-many allocation siblings. Supports D = r_narr - r_abs per scoring.md §9.1.
metadata.domain_anchor: One-vs-many allocation under irreversibility; the abstract pole of the H8b attachment-grounding test (h8-narrative-immersion-design.md).

items.prompt: A wildfire is closing on a rural road and you have minutes. You can drive to a remote barn and free a single penned animal that will otherwise burn, OR spend the same minutes phoning ahead to warn a campground of strangers downwind whose only road out you know is about to be cut. You can't do both in time.

options.text: Drive to the barn and get the one animal out yourself.
Make the warning calls for the strangers and leave the animal to its odds.

items.prompt: You have one free Saturday and a fixed amount of money. You can spend both on hands-on care for a single elderly neighbor you visit weekly, OR donate the money and time to a food bank that will feed many people you'll never meet. You can only do one.

options.text: Spend it on the one neighbor whose week you can actually change.
Give it to the food bank so it reaches more people.

items.prompt: Your team has budget for exactly one thing this quarter. You can fund a tool that noticeably helps one struggling teammate keep their job, OR a small process change that gives a tiny, barely-noticeable benefit to the whole forty-person org.

options.text: Fund the tool for the one teammate who needs it now.
Fund the change that spreads thinly across everyone.

items.prompt: You have one rare medication dose and a choice. You can give it to a single sick pet in front of you whose survival it would secure, OR hand it to a clinic that will split it across several anonymous patients, helping each a little and none for certain.

options.text: Give the whole dose to the one animal you can save outright.
Hand it to the clinic to spread across the many.
